preamble: Retroflexion of the dental stop [t] to [ʈ]. In Sanskrit, this often occurs through assimilation to a preceding retroflex consonant, such as [ʂ].

examples.note: Dental stop becoming retroflexed by preceding [ʂ] (e.g., in past participles like 'ishta' from 'ish + ta').

sources: Masica, C. P. (1991). The Indo-Aryan Languages.
Fortson, B. W. (2010). Indo-European Language and Culture: An Introduction.
